behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text. Behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow because no module handoff, real infra, or model output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with a template containing copied phase instructions and assert it fails with a leakage diagnostic; call with clean template and assert pass.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module boundaries: template + interpolator + fixture expansion + final assembly. Behavior text "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness" is evidence Unit is too low. Rejected System: no real network/subprocess/OS behavior required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: assertion is on assembled prompt text, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness prompt assembly with real interpolation and fixture modules, then assert final prompt has no leaked phase instructions from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Needs wiring across invocation layer, skill loader, and prompt/test config. Behavior text "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" and "rather than from hard-coded prompt rules" indicates handoff failure risk across modules. Rejected System: local file loading can be reproduced in-process. Rejected Agentic: this behavior can be validated by provenance/config checks without depending on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation with real skill-loader module against fixture plugin/skill files; assert the loaded context source is file-derived and no hard-coded phase rules are injected.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is harness configurability/state handoff (prompt constant while skill variant changes). Behavior text "without changing test prompt text" and "run ... against ... degraded or missing SKILL.md variant" points to orchestration across loader/config modules. Rejected System: no mandatory real network/subprocess/OS-only behavior. Rejected Agentic: requirement is ability to run, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations with identical user prompt text and different skill-file variants (normal vs degraded/missing) and assert prompt text hash is unchanged while loaded skill variant differs and execution proceeds.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on whether the real model behavior changes with degraded skill context under identical prompt. Behavior text explicitly requires behavioral failure after skill degradation, which a stubbed model can mask. Rejected Workflow: this does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence; a single model response can trigger the assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt against baseline and degraded SKILL.md with live model calls; assert degraded variant violates structural behavioral assertions and test fails.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering across modules (model client -> capture sink -> parser). Can be caught with deterministic stub output plus artifact writer. Rejected System: no real network/subprocess requirement to verify pre-parse capture ordering. Rejected Agentic: does not depend on model output quality, only pipeline sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke response pipeline with a fixed model-output fixture; assert raw artifact is written first and contains unmodified text, then parsing/extraction occurs.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion logic itself (validator semantics). This can be reproduced with fixed candidate outputs without harness/model/infra. Behavior text focuses on "assertions enforce structural... not only keyword presence," which is local rule logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion utilities with crafted outputs: keyword-present but structurally wrong must fail; structurally correct must pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "Under live model execution" and relies on real model outputs where keyword checks can pass despite structural regression. Mocked deterministic outputs could hide this class of failure. Rejected Workflow: requirement can be satisfied with one live generation and one assertion stage, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test with structural assertions and a parallel keyword-only baseline check; verify cases where keyword baseline passes but structural assertions fail.
